headerDisplay:
Bridges HomeKit and [UniFi Alarm Manager](https://help.ui.com/hc/en-us/articles/27721287753239) in both directions.

**Buttons — HomeKit → UniFi.** Momentary switches that fire an Alarm Manager webhook when turned on. Create an API key under **Settings → Control Plane → Integrations**, then in **Protect → Alarm Manager** add an alarm with a **Webhook** trigger and paste its URL below.

**Sensors — UniFi → HomeKit.** Contact/Motion/Occupancy sensors that flip when the Alarm Manager posts to this plugin, so Home app automations can react. Give each sensor a stable **ID**, then copy its ready-to-paste webhook URL from the **Webhooks** tab above (also printed in the Homebridge log at startup). In **Protect → Alarm Manager**, add an alarm and a **Webhook** action pointing at that URL.

name.title: Platform Name
name.description: The name used for this platform in the Homebridge logs.

apiKey.title: UniFi API Key
apiKey.description: Sent as the X-API-KEY header with every outgoing button webhook. Required for Integration API webhook URLs; can be overridden per button.

name.title: Name
name.description: The switch name shown in HomeKit. You can rename a button at any time — the accessory (and its automations) survives.

url.title: Webhook URL
url.description: The webhook URL copied from the Alarm Manager trigger.

method.title: HTTP Method
method.description: POST for Integration API URLs (the default), GET for legacy webhook URLs.

apiKey.title: API Key Override
apiKey.description: Overrides the global API key for this button only.

id.title: Stable ID (advanced)
id.description: Optional identity anchor. Set it before the first launch if you may rotate the webhook URL later, or if two buttons share one URL. Adding or changing it afterwards re-creates the accessory in HomeKit.

requireDoublePress.title: Require Double Press (safety)
requireDoublePress.description: Safety guard against accidental triggers: press the switch once to arm it (it stays on to show it is waiting), then press again within the confirmation window to fire the webhook. A single activation only arms the button — so a scene, automation, or Siri command that switches it on can arm it but will not fire it.

doublePressWindowSeconds.title: Confirmation Window (seconds)
doublePressWindowSeconds.description: How long you have to press a second time after the first (arming) press. Only applies when Require Double Press is on.

allowSelfSigned.title: Allow Self-Signed Certificates
allowSelfSigned.description: UniFi consoles serve a self-signed certificate on the LAN. Disable only if your console presents a trusted certificate.

resetDelayMs.title: Switch Reset Delay (milliseconds)
resetDelayMs.description: How long a button switch stays on before flipping back off after a successful trigger.

name.title: Name
name.description: The sensor name shown in HomeKit. Rename it any time — the accessory (and its automations) survives.

id.title: Stable ID
id.description: Recommended. Anchors the accessory identity and lets the plugin generate and remember the secret webhook URL for you — copy it from the Webhooks tab (also printed in the log at startup). Required unless you set an explicit Token below.

sensorType.title: Sensor Type
sensorType.description: Which HomeKit sensor to expose. All three can trigger Home app automations; contact reads as Open/Closed, motion as Detected, occupancy as Occupied.

resetDelayMs.title: Reset Delay (milliseconds)
resetDelayMs.description: How long the sensor reads "detected" after a webhook before it auto-resets.

token.title: Token (advanced)
token.description: The secret path segment of this sensor's webhook URL. Leave empty to let the plugin generate one (needs a Stable ID), or use the Webhooks tab's Generate button. Set your own only to control the URL — use a long random string.

port.title: Webhook Listener Port
port.description: Port the plugin listens on for incoming UniFi webhooks (only started when sensors are configured). Must be reachable from your UniFi console and not clash with another service.

bindHost.title: Bind Address (advanced)
bindHost.description: Network interface the listener binds to. Defaults to all interfaces so the console can reach it; set a specific IP to restrict it.

webhookSecret.title: Incoming Webhook Secret (advanced)
webhookSecret.description: Optional shared secret required on incoming webhooks, on top of each sensor's token. Add it in the UniFi webhook action's custom headers as "Authorization: Bearer <secret>" or "X-Webhook-Token: <secret>".

items.helpvalue: Momentary switches that fire a UniFi Alarm Manager webhook when turned on — from Siri, a scene, or an automation.
Contact/Motion/Occupancy sensors driven by incoming UniFi webhooks. Use the Webhooks tab above to generate each sensor's secret and copy its ready-to-paste URL.
